_provenance.method: Hindcast irrigation scheduling using degraded 5-day forecast vs perfect knowledge (Exp 014)
references: Ali, Dong & Lavely (2024) Ag Water Mgmt 306:109148
Allen et al. (1998) FAO-56 Ch 8 + Ch 10
_provenance.data_source: Synthetic Michigan weather (deterministic RNG, same as Exp 014)
_provenance._tolerance_justification: Forecast degrades with lead time; yield gap of 0-0.15 is typical for 5-day agricultural forecasts. Mass balance tolerance 0.1 mm for floating-point accumulation with stochastic forecast noise.

crop_parameters._source: FAO-56 Table 12 (corn, grain), Table 24 (Ky) — same as Exp 014

soil_parameters._source: USDA NRCS (silt loam) — same as Exp 014

thresholds._note: yield_gap = perfect - forecast. Negative means forecast overperforms (preemptive scheduling). irrigation_ratio = forecast/perfect irrigation total.
